LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS BETHEL CHRISTIAN CHURCH, TIM RAIMER, AND STEVE KORCH'S DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFFS' THIRD AMENDED COMPLAINT. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING - SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION FOR NEGLIGENCE, OVERRULED AS TO THE 2(B) CAUSE OF ACTION FOR INTENTIONAL INFLICTION, AND TAKEN OFF CALENDAR AS TO THE FIRST (ERRONEOUSLY LABELED THE FOURTH) CAUSE OF ACTION FOR ASSAULT. THE NEGLIGENCE CAUSE OF ACTION IS BARRED BY THE MINISTERIAL EXCEPTION, WHILE THE INTENTIONAL INFLICTION CLAIM IS PREDICATED IN PART UPON THE ALLEGED ASSAULT WHICH IS OUTSIDE THE MINISTERIAL EXCEPTION. DEFENDANTS WITHDREW THEIR DEMURRER AS TO THE ASSAULT CLAIM. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. IN ADDITION, THE COURT SIGNED DEFENDANTS ORDER RE MOTION TO SET ASIDE DISMISSAL RE 08/04/11 HEARING. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, REPORTER: LAVENA WARD, CSR #7077

DEMURRER TO 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-05-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
DEFENDANTS BETHEL CHRISTIAN CHURCH, TIM RAIMER, AND STEVE KORCH'S DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFFS' THIRD AMENDED COMPLAINT.

APPEARANCES: John E. Stringer, Esq. 934-1827 for pltf & opposing party; Alice Conway Powers, Esq. of Lewis Brisbois Bisgaard & Smith LLP 438-5909 for defts & moving parties.

RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling - sustained without leave to amend as to the second cause of action for negligence, overruled as to the 2(b) cause of action for intentional infliction, and taken off calendar as to the first (erroneously labeled the fourth) cause of action for assault. The negligence cause of action is barred by the ministerial exception, while the intentional infliction claim is predicated in part upon the alleged assault which is outside the ministerial exception. Defendants withdrew their demurrer as to the assault claim. Order signed in open court. In addition, the Court signed defendants order re motion to set aside dismissal re 08/04/11 hearing. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR #7077

Ntc Of Motion To Set Aside Dismissal ;
Off Calendar Aug-04-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF AARON KUMLIN, APRIL KUMLIN'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE DISMISSAL.
Appearances: John E. stringer (934.1827) for plaintiff and moving party; Alice Conway Powers of Lewis Brisbois Bisgaard & Smith LLP (438.5909) for defendants and responding parties.
Argued and the court ruled as follows: plaintiffs' motion to set aside the dismissal is granted. The court took the matter of fees under submission. (302/LMG) Judge: Loretta M. Giorgi, Reporter: Karla Ellis-Davis, CSR #12998
----
After hearing, the court ruled as follows: the court awards reasonable attorney fees and costs as required under CCP 473(b) in the amount of $2000. Request for penalties is denied. Counsel notified of the court's ruling by telephone. Prevailing party to submit a proposed form of order.

DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-16-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS BETHEL CHRISTIAN CHURCH, TIM RAIMER, STEVE KORCH'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT. Appearances: John E. Stringer (934.1827) for plaintiffs and responding parties; Alice Conway Powers of Lewis Brisbois Bisgaard & Smith (438.5909) for defendants and moving parties. Argued and the court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: sustained without leave to amend as to the 1st and 3rd causes of action. Sustained with leave to amend as to the 2nd and 2nd cause of action. Plaintiff must plead emotional distress arising from activities other than the termination and alleged defamation that led to termination, which are barred by the ministerial exception. Overruled as to the 4th cause of action. Order signed in open court. (302/PHA) Judge: Paul H. Alvarado; Reporter: Gordon Aiavao, CSR #11216

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Nov-03-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Alice Conway Powers, Esq. of Lewis Brisbois Bisgaard & Smith, 415-438-5909 for defendants and moving parties BETHEL CHRISTIAN CHURCH, TIM RAIMER, AND STEVE KORCH
John Stringer, Esq., 415-934-1827 for plaintiff and opposing party Aaron Kumlin

RULING:
Argued and the Court adopted and modified its tentative ruling:
DEFENDANTS BETHEL CHRISTIAN CHURCH, TIM RAIMER, AND STEVE KORCH'S DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. DEMURRER SUSTAINED IN PART AND OVERRULED IN PART. DEMURRER SUSTAINED WITH 10 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO NEGLIGENT INFLICTION OF EMOTIONAL DISTRESS FOR PLAINTIFF TO PLEAD THE EXISTENCE OF A DUTY AND BREACH OF DUTY. DEMURRER OVERRULED AS TO REMAINDER. PLAINTIFF TO INSERT THE NAMES OF THE INDIVIDUAL DEFENDANTS' AS TO EACH CAUSE OF ACTION THAT THE DEFENDANTS' ARE TO BE HELD LIABLE FOR. Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard, Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR #5797 (302/CWW)
